title: backfill_l3_embeddings never invoked - dense L3 retrieval had no vectors in production
current_state: T-291 deliberately left L3 write-time embedding NULL (hot path) with backfill_l3_embeddings() as the fill mechanism - but nothing in production called it. Result: retrieve()'s dense pass over L3 always saw zero embedded rows outside tests; the T-292/T-293 paraphrase-recall capability was dormant in real sessions.
target_state: Backfill runs automatically at session exit inside the existing _do_caretaker_full budgeted daemon thread (its docstring already anticipated 'a slow embedding pass'). Capped at 25 rows/exit; idempotent; offline no-ops instantly; partial progress resumes next exit.
resolution: One call added to _do_caretaker_full._run after caretaker.full(): n = agent.memory.backfill_l3_embeddings(limit=25). Wiring test asserts _do_caretaker_full invokes it; exit-path regressions (test_session_exit_retention, test_resumable_exit) stay green.
risk_notes: Daemon thread + 10s budget: a slow embed pass past budget stops being waited on and dies with the process; partial backfill is safe (idempotent, NULL-only selector). limit=25 keeps a normal exit snappy (~25 embed calls worst case).
